---
title: 'Character Sketch: Dr Richard T. Briar'
date: '2014-12-28T07:23:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4958245004943840261'
blogger_filename: '/2014/12/character-sketch-dr-richard-t-briar_27.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'White Noise'
---

<br /><br /><br /><br /><div style="border-width: 100%; direction: ltr;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 7.3916in;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 7.3916in;"><br /><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Author of:<span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp; </span>“Unlocking the Paranormal Mind. A study of<br />how the human mind’s unlocked potential”, By Dr Richard T. Briar. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Richard Briar was a<br />scientist, who specialized in the paranormal. His birth name was Niklaus von<br />Klaus</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />He was named after<br />the patron saint of Switzerland. Niklaus von Flue, also known as Brother Klaus,<br />who died in 1487. Brother Klaus was known as a spiritual advisor and pastor to<br />both rural and rich; however, was also known for heretical practices of mysticism.<br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Dr. Briar's mother<br />was a mystic from Switzerland, and his father was a German scientist during<br />WWI. Dr Niklaus von Klaus was a German Nazi scientist during WWII, who fled to<br />Switzerland, and then to the UK, after changing his name and accent. He now<br />operated under the alias of Dr. Richard Briar, tenured professor (retired) of<br />Cambridge University, and founder of the Briar Psychiatric Center, Colorado<br />Springs, CO. , dedicated to studying the farthest reaches and capabilities of<br />the human mind. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />There were exactly<br />zero people alive who knew Dr. Briar's original identity, or the extent of his<br />true plans.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />It would be<br />interesting to see just how far this rabbit hole would go…. What secrets the<br />unlocking the human mind would reveal. It was necessary to operate on the<br />fringe of the law, after all, we all know how bureaucracies hold back the<br />spirit of innovation! </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />&nbsp;******</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">Niklaus von Flue, Nicholas of Flühe</span> or <span style="font-weight: bold;">brother Klaus</span> (* <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/1417&amp;usg=ALkJrhg4R0hNoWbINKnOOAjEMvXr3wCQCw">1417</a><br />; † <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/21._M%25C3%25A4rz&amp;usg=ALkJrhg6UEvyxQlBliVx3pQUBq7XsFrTCg">21<br />March</a> <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/1487&amp;usg=ALkJrhi7SY-gS8rLYlX_1U43Bfw23nXdzQ">1487</a><br />in <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fl%25C3%25BCeli-Ranft&amp;usg=ALkJrhjcq3VhWbEfOVpDxOSDs01GmQQvlA">Flüeli</a><br />at <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sachseln&amp;usg=ALkJrhj49mLeR0yvaj4nfSGVCXKh4FE_EA">Sachseln</a><br />in the canton of <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kanton_Obwalden&amp;usg=ALkJrhjDlMjGpy611UhUIxFnsqYcm5mq6w">Obwalden</a><br />) was a <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schweiz&amp;usg=ALkJrhgC4TbYggj3r-ij8TR1ENwE5SimJw">Swiss</a><br /><a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eremit&amp;usg=ALkJrhjyV_b-ay9KOZPrgLbcQHi4IZR3sQ">hermit</a><br />, <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Askese&amp;usg=ALkJrhhzqJ4u78g2VU-KG-xKHMWZAqZnSA">ascetic</a><br />and <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mystik&amp;usg=ALkJrhiM_DYiVWRRtSulY8H2tKq099J-2A">mystic</a><br />. He is the patron saint of Switzerland.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="color: #595959; font-family: Calibri; font-size: 9.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />From<br />&lt;<a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw">http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw</a>&gt;<br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="background: #E6ECF9;">On March 21, 1487 Brother Klaus died after a hard<br />agony on the floor of his cell.</span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="color: #595959; font-family: Calibri; font-size: 9.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />From<br />&lt;<a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw">http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw</a>&gt;<br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Niklaus von Flue<br />gained widely reputation as <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seelsorger&amp;usg=ALkJrhi_leZjQbvmjg7YGcbhMl-5yToMGA">pastor</a><br />and spiritual advisor, not only for the rural population, but also as a guide<br />for foreign heads of state in Europe in the 15th century. So says a special<br />envoy of the Duchy of Milan, in a letter to <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ludovico_Sforza&amp;usg=ALkJrhhWE83ImHcF9mcR2NDffqVD0m40Yg">Ludovico<br />Sforza</a> of visits to the hermit, where he discussed political issues, and<br />the Duke thanked in the response for its gracious greetings. <a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw#cite_note-3"><span style="vertical-align: super;">[3]</span></a> </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Nicholas of Flue was<br />also interested in a mystic in worldly things. He watched the political events<br />and was asked for advice on such matters. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="color: #595959; font-family: Calibri; font-size: 9.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />From<br />&lt;<a href="http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw">http://translate.googleusercontent.com/translate_c?depth=1&amp;hl=en&amp;rurl=translate.google.com&amp;sandbox=0&amp;sl=auto&amp;tl=en&amp;u=http://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niklaus_von_Fl%25C3%25BCe&amp;usg=ALkJrhg49HZRF0nkWXbl5N4OPvyGPgXZBw</a>&gt;<br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />The origins of <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nazism">Nazism</a> and its relation to the <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Occult">occult</a> have been a part of <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Popular_culture">popular culture</a> since<br />at least 1959. There are documentaries and books on the topic, including <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Morning_of_the_Magicians"><span style="font-style: italic;">The Morning of the Magicians</span></a> (1960) and <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_Lance#Trevor_Ravenscroft"><span style="font-style: italic;">The Spear of Destiny</span></a> (1972). Historian <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nicholas_Goodrick-Clarke">Nicholas<br />Goodrick-Clarke</a> analyzed the topic in <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Occult_Roots_of_Nazism"><span style="font-style: italic;">The Occult Roots of Nazism</span></a> in which he<br />argued there were links between some ideals of <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ariosophy">Ariosophy</a> and Nazi ideology.<br />He also analyzed the problems of the numerous popular "occult<br />historiography" books written on the topic. He sought to separate <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Empiricism">empiricism</a> and <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sociology">sociology</a> from the<br />"Modern Mythology of Nazi Occultism" that exist in many books which<br />"have represented the Nazi phenomenon as the product of arcane and demonic<br />influence". He considered most of these to be "sensational and<br />under-researched".<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nazism_and_occultism#cite_note-1"><span style="vertical-align: super;">[1]</span></a></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="color: #595959; font-family: Calibri; font-size: 9.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />From<br />&lt;<a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nazism_and_occultism">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nazism_and_occultism</a>&gt;<br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />**</div><br /></div><br /></div><br /></div><br />
